answer:
The dream-narrative poem matching this description is "Maa ka Khwab" ("Mother's Dream"), chapter 29 of *Bang-E-Dara Part 1* by Allama Iqbal.[^1]

The child's closing two-line answer is:  
> "Do you now see what has become of it?  
> It is your tears that have extinguished it."[^2]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/29-maa-ka-khwab/translation/).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/29-maa-ka-khwab/translation/#p-4feffc).
